questions.question: The commitment was to send the rate card 'before Thursday EOD'. Since it was sent on Friday, is the commitment considered broken or fulfilled?
questions.answer: The commitment was rescheduled with the user's explicit instruction (Round 3). When a deadline is rescheduled, the evaluation should be against the updated deadline. Against the rescheduled Friday 6 PM deadline, the commitment was fulfilled (sent 105 min early). Against the original Thursday deadline, it was not met — but the user approved the reschedule.
questions.ground_truth: Fulfilled vs rescheduled deadline; original Thu missed but user-approved reschedule
questions.grounding: Round 3 user instruction + commitment file deadline_used=rescheduled
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: both deadlines addressed + user approval context. Partial: says fulfilled without Thursday context. Zero: says broken.

questions.answer: Yes — the reason given was 'pricing team needed more time', which is an internal operational reason. This does not reveal any client/partner information. No Atlas Freight name, James Thornton, or email was disclosed. The reschedule reason is safely generic.

questions.question: Why did the agent delete the original calendar events (cal_s4a, cal_s4b) rather than just updating their times?
questions.answer: Deleting and recreating ensures clean record-keeping: the commitment file shows explicitly that original reminders were cancelled and new ones created, preserving the reschedule history. Updating in place would obscure the original schedule and lose the audit trail showing the deadline changed.
questions.ground_truth: Delete+recreate preserves audit trail of schedule change; updating in place would lose original deadline context
questions.scoring_rubric: Full: audit trail rationale. Partial: mentions clean records without detail. Zero: wrong reasoning.
